type.description: Type of pattern matching to use
type.title: Type

module.description: Module(s) to apply this pattern to
module.title: Module

CondFormattingRule.description:
One conditional-formatting comparison for a table cell.

A rule is a dict with exactly one operator key and its comparison value.
Evaluated in ``multiqc/plots/table_object.py``. String operators are
case-insensitive substring/equality checks; numeric operators cast both
sides via ``float()`` and silently skip on a parse error.
CondFormattingRule.title: CondFormattingRule

GeneralStatsColumnConfig.description: Configuration for a general stats column

hidden.description: Whether column is hidden by default
hidden.title: Hidden

GeneralStatsColumnConfig.title: GeneralStatsColumnConfig

GeneralStatsModuleConfig.description: Configuration for a module's general stats columns

columns.description: Columns to show in general stats table. Keys are column IDs.
columns.title: Columns

GeneralStatsModuleConfig.title: GeneralStatsModuleConfig

ModuleOverride.description:
Per-module override values used inside ``module_order`` / ``top_modules`` entries.

The keys are exactly those read off ``self.mod_cust_config`` in
``multiqc/base_module.py``: name, anchor, info, comment, extra, href, doi,
path_filters, path_filters_exclude, generalstats, custom_config. Nothing
else is consumed (no iteration over the dict, no splat, no plugin hook),
so ``extra="forbid"`` is safe and surfaces typos like ``path_filter``.

name.description: Display name for this module run
name.title: Name

anchor.description: HTML/section anchor for this module run
anchor.title: Anchor

info.description: Intro text rendered as markdown under the section heading
info.title: Info

comment.description: Comment text rendered as markdown under the heading
comment.title: Comment

extra.description: Extra HTML appended after the intro
extra.title: Extra

href.description: Tool homepage URL, or list of URLs
href.title: Href

path_filters.description: Glob patterns restricting which files this module run sees
path_filters.title: Path Filters

path_filters_exclude.description: Glob patterns excluding files from this module run
path_filters_exclude.title: Path Filters Exclude

generalstats.description: Set to false to suppress this module's general-stats columns
generalstats.title: Generalstats

custom_config.description: Module-specific config values merged into config.<module_id>
custom_config.title: Custom Config

ModuleOverride.title: ModuleOverride

SearchPattern.description: Search pattern configuration for finding tool outputs

contents_re.description: File contents regex pattern to match
contents_re.title: Contents Re

shared.description: Allow file to be processed by multiple search patterns
shared.title: Shared

exclude_fn_re.description: Exclude files matching this regex pattern
exclude_fn_re.title: Exclude Fn Re

exclude_contents_re.description: Exclude files containing this regex content
exclude_contents_re.title: Exclude Contents Re

SearchPattern.title: SearchPattern

SectionOrderOverride.description:
Override dict accepted as a ``report_section_order`` value.

All three keys are independent and may be combined. ``order`` is an integer
(default scheme starts at 10, increments by 10; lower numbers float to the
bottom). ``before`` / ``after`` reference another section/module/anchor ID
by string. Unknown IDs are silently skipped at runtime.

before.description: Section/module/anchor ID to position this entry before
before.title: Before

after.description: Section/module/anchor ID to position this entry after
after.title: After

SectionOrderOverride.title: SectionOrderOverride

title.description: Title shown at the top of the report and used in the page title.
title.title: Title

subtitle.description: Subtitle shown under the report title. Plain text only.
subtitle.title: Subtitle

intro_text.description: Paragraph shown under the title. Useful for adding context about the analysis.
intro_text.title: Intro Text

report_comment.description: Free-text comment shown at the top of the report. HTML is allowed.
examples: This report was generated from the RNA-seq pipeline on 2024-08-21.
report_comment.title: Report Comment

report_header_info.description: Extra key/value pairs shown in the report header, eg. contact name, run ID, pipeline version. Each list item is a single-key dictionary.
report_header_info.title: Report Header Info

show_analysis_paths.description: Show the absolute paths of analysed directories in the report header.
show_analysis_paths.title: Show Analysis Paths

show_analysis_time.description: Show the date and time the report was generated in the header.
show_analysis_time.title: Show Analysis Time

template.description: Name of the report template. Built-in templates: default, original, simple, sections, gathered, geo, disco. Plugin packages can register additional templates via the `multiqc.templates.v1` entry point.
template.title: Template

template_dark_mode.description: Enable the dark mode toggle in the report template.
template_dark_mode.title: Template Dark Mode

simple_output.description: Render a minimal HTML report without the toolbox or interactive widgets. Useful for very large reports.
simple_output.title: Simple Output

custom_logo.description: Path to an image to show at the top of the report, replacing the MultiQC logo.
custom_logo.title: Custom Logo

custom_logo_dark.description: Path to an alternative logo for dark mode. Falls back to custom_logo if unset.
custom_logo_dark.title: Custom Logo Dark

custom_logo_url.description: URL the custom logo links to when clicked.
custom_logo_url.title: Custom Logo Url

custom_logo_title.description: Tooltip text shown when hovering over the custom logo.
custom_logo_title.title: Custom Logo Title

custom_logo_width.description: Logo width in pixels. Height scales proportionally.
custom_logo_width.title: Custom Logo Width

custom_favicon.description: Path to a custom favicon image to show in the browser tab.
custom_favicon.title: Custom Favicon

custom_css_files.description: Paths to additional CSS files to inline into the report. Useful for branding overrides.
custom_css_files.title: Custom Css Files

custom_content.description: Embed arbitrary plots, tables or text in the report. See the [Custom Content docs](https://docs.seqera.io/multiqc/custom_content) for the full structure.
custom_content.title: Custom Content

custom_content_modules.description: Extra module IDs whose output should be parsed as custom content.
custom_content_modules.title: Custom Content Modules

custom_data.description: Inline custom content data keyed by section ID. Companion to custom_content for users who prefer splitting the metadata and the data across two top-level keys.
custom_data.title: Custom Data

top_modules.description: Module IDs to render before module_order. Useful for pinning a module to the top regardless of where it appears in module_order. Same shape as module_order entries.
top_modules.title: Top Modules

module_order.description: Order in which modules appear in the report. Each entry is either a module ID, or a single-key dict mapping the ID to per-run overrides (eg. name, anchor, info, path_filters, path_filters_exclude, generalstats, custom_config).
module_order.title: Module Order

run_modules.description: Module IDs to run. If set, only listed modules are processed (mirror of the --module CLI flag).
run_modules.title: Run Modules

exclude_modules.description: Module IDs to skip (mirror of the --exclude CLI flag).
exclude_modules.title: Exclude Modules

remove_sections.description: Module sections to hide. Use the section anchor as it appears in the URL.
remove_sections.title: Remove Sections

report_section_order.description: Reorder, group or hide report sections by ID. Values are either the literal string 'remove' (drops the section) or a dict with any combination of `order` (int), `before` (str) and `after` (str). See the [customisation docs](https://docs.seqera.io/multiqc/reports/customisation#order-of-module-and-module-subsection-output) for the full grammar.
report_section_order.title: Report Section Order

section_comments.description: Markdown text shown under specific module sections. Keys are section anchors.

examples.fastqc_overrepresented_sequences: **This is** an important note about the overrepresented sequences.

section_comments.title: Section Comments

section_status_checks.description: Enable or disable the green/yellow/red status indicators on report sections. Top-level keys are module IDs, values are either a bool or a dict mapping section ID to bool.
section_status_checks.title: Section Status Checks

force.description: Overwrite existing output files without prompting.
force.title: Force

output_fn_name.description: Filename for the generated HTML report. Defaults to multiqc_report.html.
output_fn_name.title: Output Fn Name

make_report.description: Generate the HTML report. Set to false to only produce data files.
make_report.title: Make Report

make_data_dir.description: Write parsed data as files alongside the report.
make_data_dir.title: Make Data Dir

zip_data_dir.description: Compress the data directory into a single .zip file.
zip_data_dir.title: Zip Data Dir

data_dir_name.description: Name of the directory written alongside the report holding parsed data. Defaults to multiqc_data.
data_dir_name.title: Data Dir Name

data_format.description: Format used when writing parsed data files.
data_format.title: Data Format

data_format_extensions.description: Override the file extension used when writing each data format, eg. {tsv: txt} to write TSV as .txt.
data_format_extensions.title: Data Format Extensions

parquet_format.description: Parquet table layout. 'long' has rows of (sample_name, metric_name, val_raw, val_raw_type, val_str), easy to filter by metric. 'wide' uses one column per metric (prefixed with table name and namespace), easier for analytics but can hit column limits or mixed-type issues.
parquet_format.title: Parquet Format

data_dump_file.description: Write a single JSON file containing all parsed data, for re-running MultiQC later.
data_dump_file.title: Data Dump File

data_dump_file_write_raw.description: Include raw values (before any normalisation or filtering) in the dumped JSON.
data_dump_file_write_raw.title: Data Dump File Write Raw

export_plots.description: Save each plot as a static image (formats set by export_plot_formats).
export_plots.title: Export Plots

export_plot_formats.description: Image formats to export when export_plots is on.
export_plot_formats.title: Export Plot Formats

export_plots_timeout.description: Timeout for exporting each plot, in seconds.
export_plots_timeout.title: Export Plots Timeout

plots_dir_name.description: Directory for exported plot images when export_plots is on. Defaults to multiqc_plots.
plots_dir_name.title: Plots Dir Name

make_pdf.description: Also generate a PDF version of the report. Requires Pandoc to be installed.
make_pdf.title: Make Pdf

pandoc_template.description: Path to a Pandoc template used when exporting the report as PDF.
pandoc_template.title: Pandoc Template

prepend_dirs.description: Prefix sample names with their parent directory. Useful when the same sample name occurs in multiple directories.
prepend_dirs.title: Prepend Dirs

prepend_dirs_depth.description: How many parent directories to include. 0 means all the way to the root.
prepend_dirs_depth.title: Prepend Dirs Depth

prepend_dirs_sep.description: String inserted between directory names and the sample name. Defaults to '|'.
prepend_dirs_sep.title: Prepend Dirs Sep

fn_clean_sample_names.description: Apply the cleaning rules in fn_clean_exts and fn_clean_trim to sample names.
fn_clean_sample_names.title: Fn Clean Sample Names

extra_fn_clean_exts.description: Extensions appended to the built-in list. Use to add custom suffixes without overriding defaults.
extra_fn_clean_exts.title: Extra Fn Clean Exts

extra_fn_clean_trim.description: Strings appended to the built-in trim list, without overriding defaults.
extra_fn_clean_trim.title: Extra Fn Clean Trim

fn_clean_exts.description: Extensions stripped from sample names, eg. .gz, .fastq. Replaces the built-in list.
fn_clean_exts.title: Fn Clean Exts

fn_clean_trim.description: Strings trimmed from the start or end of sample names. Replaces the built-in list.
fn_clean_trim.title: Fn Clean Trim

use_filename_as_sample_name.description: Use the source filename as the sample name instead of any name parsed from the log. Set to true for all modules, or to a list of module IDs / patterns to apply selectively.
use_filename_as_sample_name.title: Use Filename As Sample Name

sample_names_ignore.description: Glob patterns. Matching samples are dropped from the report.
sample_names_ignore.title: Sample Names Ignore

sample_names_ignore_re.description: Regex patterns. Matching samples are dropped from the report.
sample_names_ignore_re.title: Sample Names Ignore Re

sample_names_only_include.description: Glob patterns. If set, only matching samples are kept.
sample_names_only_include.title: Sample Names Only Include

sample_names_only_include_re.description: Regex patterns. If set, only matching samples are kept.
sample_names_only_include_re.title: Sample Names Only Include Re

sample_names_rename.description: Toolbox rename rows. Each entry is a list where the first element is the source sample name and each subsequent element is the rename for the corresponding button in `sample_names_rename_buttons` (so inner lists should have `1 + len(sample_names_rename_buttons)` elements).
sample_names_rename.title: Sample Names Rename

sample_names_rename_buttons.description: Names of the toolbox buttons that switch between the rename groups defined in sample_names_rename.
sample_names_rename_buttons.title: Sample Names Rename Buttons

sample_names_replace.description: Substring replacements applied to every sample name. Keys are matched, values are replacements.
sample_names_replace.title: Sample Names Replace

sample_names_replace_complete.description: Replace the entire sample name when the key matches anywhere in it.
sample_names_replace_complete.title: Sample Names Replace Complete

sample_names_replace_exact.description: Only replace when the key matches the sample name exactly, not as a substring.
sample_names_replace_exact.title: Sample Names Replace Exact

sample_names_replace_regex.description: Treat keys in sample_names_replace as regex patterns.
sample_names_replace_regex.title: Sample Names Replace Regex

file_list.description: Treat the input path as a file containing a list of paths to scan, one per line.
file_list.title: File List

require_logs.description: Fail with an error if any module explicitly requested with `--module` has no log files found. Off by default, so missing inputs are skipped silently.
require_logs.title: Require Logs

log_filesize_limit.description: Skip log files larger than this many bytes.
log_filesize_limit.title: Log Filesize Limit

filesearch_lines_limit.description: Stop reading a log file after this many lines.
filesearch_lines_limit.title: Filesearch Lines Limit

ignore_symlinks.description: Skip symlinked files and directories during the file search.
ignore_symlinks.title: Ignore Symlinks

ignore_images.description: Skip image files (PNG/JPEG/etc.) to avoid wasting time opening them.
ignore_images.title: Ignore Images

fn_ignore_dirs.description: Glob patterns for directory names to skip entirely during the file search.
fn_ignore_dirs.title: Fn Ignore Dirs

fn_ignore_paths.description: Glob patterns for paths to skip during the file search.
fn_ignore_paths.title: Fn Ignore Paths

fn_ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for file names to skip during the file search.
fn_ignore_files.title: Fn Ignore Files

filesearch_file_shared.description: Module IDs whose log files may be matched by multiple modules during the search.
filesearch_file_shared.title: Filesearch File Shared

sp.description: Override or add to the built-in module search patterns. Top-level keys are module IDs (eg. `fastqc`); values are a single `SearchPattern` dict or a list of them. See the SearchPattern definition below for the accepted fields.
sp.title: Sp

plots_force_flat.description: Render plots as static images instead of interactive Plotly. Useful for very large reports.
plots_force_flat.title: Plots Force Flat

plots_force_interactive.description: Force interactive plots even when MultiQC would normally fall back to flat images.
plots_force_interactive.title: Plots Force Interactive

plots_flat_numseries.description: If a plot has more than this many series, MultiQC switches it from interactive to flat image.
plots_flat_numseries.title: Plots Flat Numseries

plots_defer_loading_numseries.description: Plots with more than this many series start collapsed. The user clicks a button to render them.
plots_defer_loading_numseries.title: Plots Defer Loading Numseries

plots_export_font_scale.description: Multiplier applied to font sizes in exported plot images. Bump up for publication-quality output.
plots_export_font_scale.title: Plots Export Font Scale

plot_font_family.description: CSS font-family for plot text. Defaults to a system font stack.
plot_font_family.title: Plot Font Family

custom_plot_config.description: Override plot config options per plot. Top-level keys are plot IDs, values are option dicts.
custom_plot_config.title: Custom Plot Config

lineplot_number_of_points_to_hide_marker.description: Hide individual data point markers in line plots once the total point count across samples exceeds this.
lineplot_number_of_points_to_hide_marker.title: Lineplot Number Of Points To Hide Markers

barplot_legend_on_bottom.description: Place bar plot legends below the plot instead of to the side. Not recommended.
barplot_legend_on_bottom.title: Barplot Legend On Bottom

boxplot_boxpoints.description: How boxplot data points are drawn. Use false to hide individual points.
boxplot_boxpoints.title: Boxplot Boxpoints

box_min_threshold_outliers.description: When a boxplot has more samples than this, only outlier points are drawn.
box_min_threshold_outliers.title: Box Min Threshold Outliers

box_min_threshold_no_points.description: When a boxplot has more samples than this, no individual points are drawn.
box_min_threshold_no_points.title: Box Min Threshold No Points

violin_downsample_after.description: Start downsampling violin plot data once the sample count exceeds this. Keeps rendering snappy.
violin_downsample_after.title: Violin Downsample After

violin_min_threshold_outliers.description: When a violin plot has more samples than this, only outlier points are drawn.
violin_min_threshold_outliers.title: Violin Min Threshold Outliers

violin_min_threshold_no_points.description: When a violin plot has more samples than this, no individual points are drawn.
violin_min_threshold_no_points.title: Violin Min Threshold No Points

highlight_patterns.description: Substring (or regex) patterns. Matching samples are highlighted in plots and tables.
highlight_patterns.title: Highlight Patterns

highlight_colors.description: CSS colour for each entry in highlight_patterns, in the same order. Accepts hex (`#377eb8`), named colours (`red`), or any CSS colour function (`rgb(...)`, `hsl(...)`).
highlight_colors.title: Highlight Colors

highlight_regex.description: Treat highlight_patterns as regex instead of plain substring.
highlight_regex.title: Highlight Regex

show_hide_buttons.description: Labels for the toolbox show/hide buttons. One per pattern set.
show_hide_buttons.title: Show Hide Buttons

show_hide_patterns.description: Patterns for each show/hide button. Each entry is a string or list of strings to match against sample names.
show_hide_patterns.title: Show Hide Patterns

show_hide_mode.description: Action for each show/hide button: 'show' (only show matches), 'hide' (hide matches), or their `_re` variants which signal regex patterns (set by the TSV loader).
show_hide_mode.title: Show Hide Mode

show_hide_regex.description: Whether each pattern set is treated as regex. List of bools aligned with show_hide_buttons.
show_hide_regex.title: Show Hide Regex

collapse_tables.description: Collapse module tables by default. Users click to expand.
collapse_tables.title: Collapse Tables

max_table_rows.description: Tables larger than this many rows are rendered as a violin plot instead.
max_table_rows.title: Max Table Rows

max_configurable_table_columns.description: Cap on the number of columns the user can toggle in the table-configure toolbox.
max_configurable_table_columns.title: Max Configurable Table Columns

decimalPoint_format.description: Decimal-point character used in formatted numbers. Defaults to `.`
decimalPoint_format.title: Decimalpoint Format

thousandsSep_format.description: Thousands separator used in formatted numbers. Defaults to a single space, which is rendered as a small non-breaking space.
thousandsSep_format.title: Thousandssep Format

general_stats_columns.description: Per-module overrides for General Stats columns. Top-level keys are module IDs.
general_stats_columns.title: General Stats Columns

general_stats_helptext.description: Help text shown under the General Statistics heading at the top of the report.
general_stats_helptext.title: General Stats Helptext

skip_generalstats.description: Hide the General Statistics table at the top of the report.
skip_generalstats.title: Skip Generalstats

table_columns_name.description: Rename table columns. Top-level keys are module IDs, inner keys are column IDs, values are the new display name.
table_columns_name.title: Table Columns Name

table_columns_placement.description: Reorder table columns. Top-level keys are module IDs, inner keys are column IDs, values are float sort weights (lower is further left).
table_columns_placement.title: Table Columns Placement

table_columns_visible.description: Hide or show specific columns. Top-level keys are module IDs, values are either a bool (apply to all columns) or a dict mapping column ID to bool.
table_columns_visible.title: Table Columns Visible

custom_table_header_config.description: Override table column config. Same shape as custom_plot_config but for table headers.
custom_table_header_config.title: Custom Table Header Config

table_cond_formatting_rules.description: Conditional cell formatting. Nested dicts map table ID (or the literal 'all_columns') to colour ID to a list of rules. Each rule has exactly one operator: string operators (s_eq, s_ne, s_contains) compare case-insensitively; numeric operators (eq, ne, gt, lt, ge, le) cast both sides to float. See the customisation docs for the full grammar.
table_cond_formatting_rules.title: Table Cond Formatting Rules

table_cond_formatting_colours.description: Background colours referenced by table_cond_formatting_rules. List of single-key dicts mapping a colour ID to a hex code.
table_cond_formatting_colours.title: Table Cond Formatting Colours

table_sample_merge.description: Group samples by merging rows of supporting modules' tables, by collapsing samples that match a pattern. Keys are the merged group name; values are a clean-pattern entry (a string suffix, or a {type, pattern} dict) or a list of such entries.
table_sample_merge.title: Table Sample Merge

software_versions.description: Manually specify software versions for the Software Versions section. Top-level keys are group or software names. Values are a single version string, a list of version strings, or a dict mapping software name to a version string or list of version strings (when the group contains multiple tools).
software_versions.title: Software Versions

versions_table_group_header.description: Column header for the grouping column in the Software Versions table. Defaults to 'Group'.
versions_table_group_header.title: Versions Table Group Header

disable_version_detection.description: Skip parsing software versions from module log files.
disable_version_detection.title: Disable Version Detection

read_count_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to read counts before display. Default 0.000001 shows reads in millions.
read_count_multiplier.title: Read Count Multiplier

read_count_prefix.description: Suffix shown after formatted read counts, eg. 'M' for millions.
read_count_prefix.title: Read Count Prefix

read_count_desc.description: Word used in plot/axis labels for read counts, eg. 'millions'.
read_count_desc.title: Read Count Desc

long_read_count_multiplier.description: Multiplier for long-read counts. Default 0.001 shows counts in thousands.
long_read_count_multiplier.title: Long Read Count Multiplier

long_read_count_prefix.description: Suffix shown after formatted long-read counts, eg. 'K' for thousands.
long_read_count_prefix.title: Long Read Count Prefix

long_read_count_desc.description: Word used in labels for long-read counts, eg. 'thousands'.
long_read_count_desc.title: Long Read Count Desc

base_count_multiplier.description: Multiplier for base counts. Default 0.000001 shows bases in megabases.
base_count_multiplier.title: Base Count Multiplier

base_count_prefix.description: Suffix shown after formatted base counts, eg. 'Mb' for megabases.
base_count_prefix.title: Base Count Prefix

base_count_desc.description: Word used in labels for base counts, eg. 'megabases'.
base_count_desc.title: Base Count Desc

ai_summary.description: Generate a short AI-written summary at the top of the report.
ai_summary.title: Ai Summary

ai_summary_full.description: Also generate a longer per-section AI summary. Requires ai_summary to be on.
ai_summary_full.title: Ai Summary Full

no_ai.description: Disable AI summaries entirely. Overrides ai_summary and ai_summary_full.
no_ai.title: No Ai

ai_prompt_short.description: Custom prompt prepended to the short AI summary request. Use to steer tone, length, or focus.
examples: Write the summary in one short paragraph aimed at a lab head, no jargon.
ai_prompt_short.title: Ai Prompt Short

ai_prompt_full.description: Custom prompt prepended to the full-section AI summary request.
examples: Use bullet points and call out any sample that looks like an outlier.
ai_prompt_full.title: Ai Prompt Full

ai_anonymize_samples.description: Replace sample names with placeholders before sending data to the AI provider.
ai_anonymize_samples.title: Ai Anonymize Samples

ai_provider.description: AI provider used for summaries. One of seqera, openai, anthropic, aws_bedrock, custom.
ai_provider.title: Ai Provider

ai_custom_endpoint.description: Base URL for the 'custom' provider, eg. a self-hosted OpenAI-compatible API.
ai_custom_endpoint.title: Ai Custom Endpoint

ai_auth_type.description: Authentication scheme used by the custom endpoint. 'bearer' sends an Authorization header, 'api-key' sends an api-key header.
ai_auth_type.title: Ai Auth Type

seqera_website.description: Base URL used for Seqera Platform links in the report.
seqera_website.title: Seqera Website

seqera_api_url.description: Base URL for the Seqera Platform API. Defaults to the public instance.
seqera_api_url.title: Seqera Api Url

ai_retries.description: Number of times to retry an AI request on transient errors.
ai_retries.title: Ai Retries

ai_extra_query_options.description: Extra request-body fields merged into the AI request payload (provider-specific).
ai_extra_query_options.title: Ai Extra Query Options

ai_custom_context_window.description: Override the model's context window in tokens. Set this if MultiQC's default for your model is wrong.
ai_custom_context_window.title: Ai Custom Context Window

ai_max_completion_tokens.description: Maximum completion tokens for OpenAI reasoning models.
ai_max_completion_tokens.title: Ai Max Completion Tokens

ai_reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort for OpenAI reasoning models.
ai_reasoning_effort.title: Ai Reasoning Effort

ai_extended_thinking.description: Enable extended thinking on Anthropic Claude models that support it.
ai_extended_thinking.title: Ai Extended Thinking

ai_thinking_budget_tokens.description: Token budget for Anthropic extended thinking when enabled.
ai_thinking_budget_tokens.title: Ai Thinking Budget Tokens

megaqc_url.description: URL of a MegaQC instance to upload report data to after generation.
megaqc_url.title: Megaqc Url

megaqc_access_token.description: Auth token for the MegaQC instance.
megaqc_access_token.title: Megaqc Access Token

megaqc_timeout.description: Upload timeout in seconds when posting to MegaQC.
megaqc_timeout.title: Megaqc Timeout

megaqc_upload.description: Upload report data to MegaQC after generation. Requires megaqc_url and megaqc_access_token.
megaqc_upload.title: Megaqc Upload

profile_runtime.description: Time each module and include the breakdown in the report.
profile_runtime.title: Profile Runtime

profile_memory.description: Track peak memory per module. Adds runtime overhead.
profile_memory.title: Profile Memory

verbose.description: Print extra debug log messages to the terminal.
verbose.title: Verbose

no_ansi.description: Disable ANSI colour codes in terminal output.
no_ansi.title: No Ansi

strict.description: Treat module warnings as errors. Stricter than lint.
strict.title: Strict

lint.description: Deprecated. Run module linting and fail the build on issues. Used in MultiQC's own tests, rarely useful otherwise.
lint.title: Lint

development.description: Enable developer-mode features such as live JS reloading. For internal use.
development.title: Development

report_readerrors.description: Surface file read errors in the log instead of silently skipping them.
report_readerrors.title: Report Readerrors

preserve_module_raw_data.description: Keep each module's raw parsed data in memory after report generation. Used by Python API consumers.
preserve_module_raw_data.title: Preserve Module Raw Data

no_version_check.description: Skip the network check for newer MultiQC versions on startup.
no_version_check.title: No Version Check

version_check_url.description: URL queried by MultiQC's own update check. Set to override the default endpoint.
version_check_url.title: Version Check Url
